_description: Final estimates — Switzerland vs Colombia R16 2026-07-07/08

ftr.source: ELO Poisson model (SUI=2005.51, COL=2090.75, neutral) — Smarkets FTR not captured pre-match
ftr.note: Smarkets FTR should be checked at kick-off; these are model-only estimates. Expect market to be in range SUI 25-32%, Draw 26-30%, COL 40-48%.

estimates.text: Will Dan Ndoye (Switzerland, #11) score a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: Ndoye is SUI's top winger/shooter. 25% goal-share assumed. RULE8 slight pull up.

estimates.text: Will Switzerland keep a clean sheet in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: P(COL=0 goals) from Dixon-Coles. SUI has only conceded 2 goals in 4 WC2026 matches. COL blanked vs POR 0-0. Conservative upward shade.

estimates.text: Will the match be tied at halftime?
estimates.note: P(HT tied) from HT Poisson model (λ_SUI_HT=0.428, λ_COL_HT=0.583). COL slightly more likely to lead at HT. Shade model slightly down.

estimates.text: Will there be 4 or more total cards shown in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: λ_cards=2.8 (SUI ~1.0 + COL ~1.5 + knockout intensity boost). P(4+ at λ=2.8)≈0.308. RULE8 shade up from base 0.242.

estimates.text: Will Colombia have 5 or more corner kicks in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: COL corners avg 4.5 across WC2026. λ=4.5 → P(5+)=0.468. COL is an attack-heavy team that earns corners. RULE8 slight shade up.

estimates.text: Will Switzerland have 4 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: SUI SOT avg 6.0 in groups; adjusted to λ=4.5 vs stronger COL (RULE12: removed Canada outlier context). P(4+ at λ=4.5)=0.658. RULE8 shade DOWN from 0.658. SUI rarely drops below 4 SOT except vs dominant teams.

estimates.text: Will a substitute score or assist a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: Tournament base rate ~31%. Both teams have effective sub options. Impact subs common in R16.

estimates.text: Will the first goal of the match be scored in the second half of regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: P(0 goals in HT) × P(>0 goals in 2H) = 0.364 × 0.709 = 0.258. Compact first half typical in R16 matches. Model estimate retained.

estimates.text: Will either team win both halves in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: Model 0.165 from joint HT/2H win probabilities. RULE8 shade up from 0.165. Rare but plausible if COL dominate or SUI score early then hold. Final: 0.20.

estimates.text: Will the match be decided by exactly one goal in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: P(|margin|=1) = 0.404 from DC grid (all 1-goal margin scorelines summed). RULE8 shade up. Tight knockouts often 1-goal margins. Final: 0.42.

estimates.text: Will at least one card be shown in each half of regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: P(card each half) ≈ 0.505 at λ_cards=2.5. Right at 0.50 threshold. Historical rate ~50-55% in WC knockouts. Final: 0.50.

estimates.text: Will Luis Díaz (Colombia, #7) score a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: Díaz is COL's primary attacker. λ_Díaz=0.350, P=0.295. His goal-share ~27% of COL expected output. Liquid market. SUI defense is solid (2 conceded in 4 games) but Díaz is elite. Final: 0.30.

estimates.text: Will Jhon Arias (Colombia, #11) score or assist a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: PLAYER_ILLIQUID: apply RULE_ILLIQUID discount. Model 0.420 shaded down ~10%. Arias is active but not primary goal threat. Score-or-assist scope is wider which helps. Final: 0.38.

estimates.text: Will Colombia advance to the quarterfinals?
estimates.note: P(COL win regulation)=0.438 + P(draw)*0.45 ET/pens factor. COL is the moderate favorite. If draw, they have ~45% to win ET/pens. Total ≈ 0.571. Model and estimate aligned. Final: 0.57.

estimates.text: Will Breel Embolo (Switzerland, #7) have 1 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
estimates.note: Embolo is SUI's central striker. λ_SUI=0.951 adjusted for neutral vs COL. At 50% SOT share: λ_Embolo_SOT≈0.476, P(1+ SOT)=0.379. Consistent with SUI-ALG market for Embolo (was 0.325 for scorer vs 0.379 for 1+ SOT here). Final: 0.38.

key_strategic_notes: Q6 (SUI 4+ SOT at 0.63) is our highest-confidence estimate — SUI is a high-SOT team even as underdogs, and 4 is a low threshold given their avg of 6.0.
Q14 (COL advance 0.57) — this is the 'meta' question; if we are right that COL wins, all COL-related props have positive correlation.

Q2 (SUI clean sheet 0.28) has value if the crowd underestimates SUI's defensive quality — crowd may anchor to COL's attacking volume (19+ shots/game) without considering COL's conversion rate (0 goals vs Portugal despite 24 shots).
Q3 (HT tied 0.45) — in compact knockout matches HT ties are common. If crowd prices this below 0.40, there may be upside.
Q4 (4+ cards 0.30) — knockout pressure increases card counts. Crowd may under-price this.
Player questions (Ndoye Q1, Díaz Q12, Embolo Q15) are all near 0.22-0.38 range where suppression vs crowd overconfidence matters most.
